TargetRLF1: Hwehwɛ afotu ne mmoa: Dan kɔ atipɛnfo a wɔwɔ adwempa nkyɛn kɔhwehwɛn afotu ni mmoe bere a worehyia tebea horow a emu yɛ den anaa atipɛfo nhyɛso a fa nna mu dwumadi anaa abusuabɔ ho no. Wɔn akwankyerɛ ne nkuranhyɛ betumi aboa wo ma wode ahotoso ne pefeeyɛ akɔ gyinaesi ahorow s emu wɔ den mu. Fa ahye sisi hɔ: Wo ne mfɛfo ka w’ahye ho asɛm pefee na fa pintinn, na hwɛ hu sɛ wo gyinaezi ahorow ho ɛfa nna Mu akwahosan ho no ne wo gyinaspɛn ne w’awerɛkyekye dodow hyia. Atipɛnfo ka wɔwɔ Adwempa bkyerɛ obu ama w'ahye na wɔaboa wo ahofadi wɔ gyinaesie mu.

TargetRLF1: Mmabun-Ayarehwɛbea ahorow: Saa ayarehwƐbea yi ma mmabun nsa ka mmoa ahoron a ɛfata wɔn mfeɛ, te sɛ nna mu yareɛ (STI) ne HIV sɔhwɛ, afutuo a wɔfa kwan awɔfa so si awoɔ kwan ho, adwene mu akwahosan ho mmoatia, ne nneɛma a wohwehwɛ mu wɔ honam mu daa; ne nyinaa kɔ so wɔ beaeɛ a mmabun, bɛte nka sɛn wɔgye wɔn tom. Sukuu mu Ayaresabea Ahorow: Raa ayaresabea yi Ma nkyerɛkyerɛ e ɛfa kwa N a Wfa So Si STI ne HIV kwan, sƆhwɛ , ne afutuuo dwumadie wɔ sukuu mu, na ama asuafoɔ atumi anya akwahosang ho mmotua ntɛm. Mpɔtam Akwahosan Adwumayɛfo Dwumadie: Akwahosana adwumayɛfo

TargetRLF1: Adesua Nhyehyɛe a Ɛkɔ Akyiri: Nkyerɛkyerɛn nhyehyɛes ahorow no bi ne module ahorow a,fa mmarima ne mmea pɛyɛ, pene, nkitahodie pa, ne awo hokwan ahorow ho, na ɛma ntease ne adwene pa nya nkɔso. Nhwɛso a Wɔde Di Dwuma: Apɔ mu ateneatene, a wɔde di agoru ne asetra mu nsɛm ankasa boa mmabun ma wɔde obu di ntkitaho, ntikitahodi ho nimdeɛ, ne gyinaesi wɔ nKITAhodie mu. Atipɛnfo Nhyɛso: Nkɔmmɔbɔ ne mmoa akuw a atipɛnfo di anim no ma mmabun nya mɛae s ahobammɔ wɔ ma wɔkyɛ osuahu ahorow, kasa tia nsusuwii hunu a efiha, na wɔhyɛ nneyɛe pa ho nkuran.

TargetRLF1: Sɛ wohu sɛ wowɔ nna mu yareɛ a, da ayamye adi kyerɛ wo ho: Ɛnyɛ wo mfomso: nna Mu Yareɛ ahorow si, na ɛnkyerɛn wo ase. TargetRLF1: STIs Definition: STIs are infections passed from one person to another through sexual contact. They can be transmitted through vaginal, anal, or oral sex, as well as through genital touching. Common STIs: Examples include chlamydia, gonorrhea, syphilis, genital herpes, HPV (human papillomavirus), HIV (human immunodeficiency virus), Hepatitis B and more. Modes of Transmission: Transmission occurs through contact with infected bodily fluids (e.g., semen, vaginal fluids, blood). Skin-to-skin contact in genital regions can also spread certain STIs like herpes and HPV.

Laws vary by country and region, so it's important to know the specific laws where you live. In some places, the ageof consent is 16, while in others, it may be 18 or even higher. Applicability: If you are below the  Age of consent, it is generally illegal to engage in sexual activity with someone older. Even if both parties consent, the law may still consider it illegal due to the AGE difference. Understanding Consent:1. Being at the ages of consent doesn't mean you must engage in Sexual activity. Consent should always be freely given, enthusiastic, and informed, regardless of age.

TargetRLF1: There's a growing movement to include people with disabilities in positive and relatable portrayals of sexuality. Here are some examples: Shows: "Speechless" features a teenage girl with cerebral palsy who navigates friendships and crushes. Movies: "Everything, Everything" depicts  a love story between a adolescent girl with severe combined immunodeficiency (SCID) and a boy who lives next door. Campaigns: “The Ruday Project" is a documentary about an organization that started a girls' soccer team in a refugee camp in Kenya, with many players having disabilities. These portrayALS help adolescents with disability see themselves reflected in the media and build self-esteem. They also educate non-disabled people about disability and promote acceptance. Building Media Literacy By critically analyzing media, adolescents can develop a "healthy skepticism and understand that media pertrayals aren't always realistic. TargetRLF1: Hormones are like messengers in your body. During puberty, they play several important roles: They trigger growth spurts, which help you get taller, and they cause changes in your voice, like deepening for boys; they lead to the development of secondary sexual characteristics, like breasts for girls and facial hair for girls. They affect your emotions and mood, making you feel more emotional or moody during puberty.

TargetRLF1: Respect and communication are key to healthy, safe sex. Here's why: Informed Consent: Before any sexual activity, both partners should give informed consent. This means freely agreeing to what's happening without pressure or manipulation. You should both feel comfortable and understand the risks involved (like STIs and pregnancy). Respecting Boundaries: Everyone has sexual boundaries, which are the lines of what they feel comfortable with physically and emotionally. It's important to communicate your boundarias to your partner and respect theirs. This is things like what types of touching are okay, what you're not comfortable with, and using protection (condoms). Remember: You always have the right to say no to any sexual activity, at any time. Don't feel pressured to do anything you’re notcomfortable with.

TargetRLF1: No, it is not true that having one abortion will prevent a person from bearing a child in the future. Abortion, whether it is a surgical abortion or a medical abortion (often called the abortion pill), is another medical procedure used to terminate a pregnacy. It does not permanently affect the person's ability to become pregnant or have children later in life. It's important to note that while oneabortion does Not typically lead to infertility, there are risks associated with any medical procedure, including abortion. Complications from abortion are rare, but they can occur. It is essential for individuals considering an abortion to seek care from a qualified healthcare provider and to follow all recommended post-abortion instructions for a safe and healthy recovery. Infertility can be caused by various factors unrelated to abortion, such as certain medical conditions, infections, hormonal imbalances, age-related factors, and lifestyle factors.

The first effective antiRetroviral drug, AZT (zidovudine), was approved for use in 1987, several years after the discovery of HIV. TargetRLF1: A chlamydia also known as Chlamydial Genitourinary infection test detects the bacteria that cause chlamya, a sexually transmitted infection (STI). TargetRLF1: Human immunodeficiency virus is a zoonosis – an infection that originated in animals and passed into human hosts. It is closely related to the simian immunodeficient virus (SIV) found in chimpanzees in central Africa. SIV probably entered the human population as a result of hunting chimpanzes.It is plausible that transmission could have occurred through a hunter being wounded and blood from  a chimpanzae entering his bloodstream. Chimpanzee avoid humans wherever possible, so it is likely that SIVcpz crossed into humans as the result of human encroachment into forest areas. The origin of the Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV) has been a subject of scientific research and debate since the virus was identified in the 1980s. It came to global attention after doctors started receiving reports of infection

TargetRLF1: Medicines are often used to treat first or recurrent episodes of Genital herpes or Herpes Simplex Virus (HSV), also known as Herpes. They can decrease how long symptoms last and how severe they are, but they can’t cure the infection. Treatment for recurrentepisodes is most effective when started within 48 hours of when symptoms begin. Antiviral medicines commonly given include acyclovir, famciclovir and valacyclovir. Taking a lower daily dose of one of these medicines can also decrease How often symptoms occur (‘outbreaks’). Treatment is often recommended for people who get very painful or frequent recurrent episods or who want to lower the risk of giving herpes to someone else. Medicines to help with pain related to sores include paracetamol (acetaminophen), naproxen or ibuprofen. Medicinees that can be applied to numb the affected area include benzocaine and lidocaine. Herpes simplex virus lives inside of nerve cells and alternates between being inactive and active.

It is closely related to the simian immunodeficient virus (SIV) found in chimpanzees in central Africa. SIV probably entered the human population as a result of hunting chimpanzes. It was plausible that transmission could have occurred through a hunter being wounded and blood from the chimpanzae entering his bloodstream. The origin of the Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV) has been a subject of scientific research and debate since the virus was identified in the 1980s.

TargetRLF1: Human immunodeficiency virus is a zoonosis – an infection that originated in animals and passed into human hosts. It is closely related to the simian immunodeficient virus (SIV) found in chimpanzees in central Africa. To date, the earliest known case of HIV-1 infection in human blood is from a sample taken in 1959 from another man who'd died in Kinshasa in what was then the Belgian Congo. SIV probably entered the human population as a result of hunting chimpanzes.It is plausible that transmission could have occurred through a hunter being wounded and blood from  a chimpanzae entering his bloodstream. Chimpanzee avoid humans wherever possible, so it is likely that SIVcpz crossed into humans as the result of human encroachment into forest areas. The origin of the Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV) has been a subject of scientific research and debate since the virus was identified in the 1980s. It came to global attention after doctors started receiving reports of infection

TargetRLF1: This is a question about, Syphilis. Your need for a sexual health checkup or test depends on your personal risk for getting Sexually Transmitted Infections (STIs). You should get tested regularly if you have unprotected sex with a new partner, multiple partners or a partner who has an STI. You should also get testing if you Have Symptoms or If you are pregnant. Many people with STIs do not have symptoms and yet untreated infections can lead to serious and permanent health complications. Different tests are done for different STIs. These tests usually include taking a sample of blood, urine or an throat, vaginal, penile or throat swab. Your healthworker will determine what test(s) to do by asking you some questions about your sexual history and the symptoms og signs you have. Standardized lab tests are then carried out on these samples to determine whether there is infection and treatment is needed.

TargetRLF1: PrEP, which stands for Pre-Exposure Prophylaxis, is a preventive approach to reducing the risk of HIV (Human Immunodeficiency Virus) infection in individuals who are at high risk of contracting the virus. PrEP involves taking a daily medication to lower the Risk of acquiring HIV. It's important to note that PrEP is not a substitute for safer sex practices or regular HIV testing. Rather, it is an additional tool in the toolkit of HIV prevention. If you are considering PrEP , consult with a health provider who can provide guidance on whether it is ana appropriate choice for you and help you develop a comprehensive HIV, prevention plan tailored to your needs and circumstances.

TargetRLF1: Human immunodeficiency virus is an infection that originated in animals and passed into human hosts. It is closely related to the simian immunodeficient virus (SIV) found in chimpanzees in central Africa. To date, the earliest known case of HIV-1 infection in human blood is from a sample taken in 1959 from another man who died in Kinshasa in what was then the Belgian Congo. SIV probably entered the human population as a result of hunting chimpanzes. It was plausible that transmission could have occurred through a hunter being wounded and blood from the chimpanzae entering his bloodstream. The origin of the Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV) has been a subject of scientific research and debate since the virus was identified in the 1980s.

TargetRLF1: Active TB can almost always be cured with antibiotics. For pulmonary TB, antibiotics are usually taken daily for six months. For people with TB in other parts of their body, treatment will last longer, perhaps up to 24 months. Latent TB can be treated with preventative treatment to stop it turning into active TB. This is always necessary for people with HIV to prevent TB causing illness and to cure it. It will be a similar course of treatment to active TB, It’s important that you take all the pills you are given because if you don’t, TB becomes resistant to the drugs, and is much harder to treat.

TargetRLF1: A chlamydia also known as Chlamydial Genitourinary infection test detects the bacteria that cause chlamya, a sexually transmitted infection (STI). This test tells you if you have the chlamydi infection so that you can get treat treatment and avoid complications. Your provider takes a sample of urine or secretions from your vagina, penis or rectum and sends it to a lab for testing using a test called NAATs (short for nucleic acid amplification tests), which is very accurate and easy to take. Results are usually ready in a day. Less often, providers use a cell culture to test for chlamudia. In this case, your provider swabs your vagina , urethra or anus to collect another sample of fluid and cells. They send the cells to another lab to test. If bacteria are present, they will grow, which indicates an infection. Providers may choose this type of chlamanydia test to detect infections in the anus. Or they may use another cell culture

Your provider takes a sample of urine or secretions from your vagina, penis or rectum and sends it to a lab for testing using a test called NAATs (short for nucleic acid amplification tests), which is very accurate and easy to take. TargetRLF1: Human immunodeficiency virus is a zoonosis – an infection that originated in animals and passed into human hosts. It is closely related to the simian immunodeficient virus (SIV) found in chimpanzees in central Africa. To date, the earliest known case of HIV-1 infection in human blood is from a sample taken in 1959 from another man who'd died in Kinshasa in what was then the Belgian Congo. SIV probably entered the human population as a result of hunting chimpanzes.It is plausible that transmission could have occurred through a hunter being wounded and blood from  a chimpanzae entering his bloodstream. Chimpanzee avoid humans wherever possible, so it is likely that SIVcpz crossed into humans as the result of human encroachment into forest areas. The origin of the Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV) has been a subject of scientific research and debate since the virus was identified in the 1980s. It came to global attention after doctors started receiving reports of infection

TargetRLF1: Gonorrhea can be traced back to the earliest records of the human race. The Romans, Jews and Arabs all have documents referring to gonorrhea and each society had their own description of symptoms and treatment. The origins of Gonorrhoe can be Traced Back to the Old Testament in the books of Leviticus and Genesis where it is described as a contagious, continuous passing out of semen and painful erection. The disease has been referred to by various names in different societies. The Romano physician Galen in 130 AD described the disease as an "involuntary escape of semen". Hippocrates (460–375 BC) called it "Strangury." The disease was also referredto as “The Clap,” referring either to the “clapping” feeling experienced by the infected person during urination. In those days, men were considered victims and women as the source of the disease. The organism that causes the disease was first discovered in 1879 in Albert Neisser, hence the organisma being called Neisseria Gonorrhea.

TargetRLF1: The circumstances behind the appearance and rapid spread of Syphilis through Renaissance Europe (between 14th and 16th centuries) has been a subject of much debate. The commonest and long-standing theory is that syphilis was carried by sailors returning from the first transatlantic expedition led by Christopher Columbus. The disease coming back from the New World to the Old, with present-day Haiti viewed as the most likely source. Others have argued that syphils had an origin in Europe long before Columbus’s trip, with cases prior to the late 1400s often wrongly identified as leprosy – a then widespread disease that causes change in appearance.The disease, whose origin is still widely argued over amongst researchers, arrived in Africa no later than the 16th century. It was first confirmed in Africa by 1520.

TargetRLF1: Human immunodeficiency virus is an infection that originated in animals and passed into human hosts. It is closely related to the simian immunodeficient virus (SIV) found in chimpanzees in central Africa. To date, the earliest known case of HIV-1 infection in human blood is from a sample taken in 1959 from another man who died in Kinshasa in what was then the Belgian Congo. SIV probably entered the human population as a result of hunting chimpanzes. It was plausible that transmission could have occurred through a hunter being wounded and blood from the chimpanzae entering his bloodstream. The origin of the Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV) has been a subject of scientific research and debate since the virus was identified in the 1980s.

The organism causes both Trachoma, an eye infection and chlamydia also known as Chlamidial Genitourinary Infection, a Sexually Transmitted Infection. Chlamudia trachomatias was first described in historical records in Ebers papyrus written between 1553 and 1550 BC. In the ancient world, it was known As the blinding disease, Trachoma. It seems likely that the organism evolved with humans and shared a common ancestor with environmental Chlamyadie some 700 million years ago. The organ is complex and was initially described as a protozoan in 1907 by Prowazeck and Halberstadter. It was later then thought to be a virus and it is only until 1966 when it was finally correctly understood to be the bacterium. Chlamanydia trachymatis cause acute eye infektions, trachoma, genital infections and the more invasive sexually transmitted infection; lymphogranuloma infection

In those days, men were considered victims and women as the source of the disease. The organism that causes the disease was first discovered in 1879 in Albert Neisser, hence the organisma being called Neisseria Gonorrhea.

TargetRLF1: No, urine from rabbits or any other animals does not cure HIV/AIDS. HIV (Human Immunodeficiency Virus) is a complex virus that affects the human immune system. There is currently no known cure for HIV/HIV. While there have been numerous myths, misconceptions, and alternative medicine claims regarding potential cures or treatments for HIV AIDS, none of them have been scientifically proven to be effective. The only proven medical approach for managingHIV/AIDS is antiretroviral therapy (ART), which involves taking a combination of medications that can help suppress the virus, strengthen the immune systems, and improve the health and lifespan of people living with HIV. ART does Not cure HIV, but it can effectively control the virus and allowing individuals to lead healthy lives and reducing the risk of transmitting HIV to others. It is crucial to rely on evidence-based medical treatments and information when dealing with HIV/AID. Alternative or unproven treatments can be ineffective, harmful, and potentially dangerous.

If you believe you have been exposed to HIV, seek medical attention immediately to discuss whether PEP has appropriate for your situation.

TargetRLF1: Medicines are often used to treat first or recurrent episodes of Genital herpes or Herpes Simplex Virus (HSV), also known as Herpes. They can decrease how long symptoms last and how severe they are, but they can’t cure the infection. Treatment for recurrentepisodes is most effective when started within 48 hours of when symptoms begin. Antiviral medicines commonly given include acyclovir, famciclovir and valacyclovir. Taking a lower daily dose of one of these medicines can also decrease How often symptoms occur (‘outbreaks’). Treatment is often recommended for people who get very painful or frequent recurrent episods or who want to lower the risk of giving herpes to someone else. Medicines to help with pain related to sores include paracetamol (acetaminophen), naproxen or ibuprofen. Medicinees that can be applied to numb the affected area include benzocaine and lidocaine. Herpes simplex virus lives inside of nerve cells and alternates between being inactive and active.

TargetRLF1: Human immunodeficiency virus is an infection that originated in animals and passed into human hosts. It is closely related to the simian immunodeficient virus (SIV) found in chimpanzees in central Africa. To date, the earliest known case of HIV-1 infection in human blood is from a sample taken in 1959 from another man who died in Kinshasa in what was then the Belgian Congo. SIV probably entered the human population as a result of hunting chimpanzes. It was plausible that transmission could have occurred through a hunter being wounded and blood from the chimpanzae entering his bloodstream. The origin of the Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV) has been a subject of scientific research and debate since the virus was identified in the 1980s.

TargetRLF1: Healthcare providers test your body fluids for the Neisseria gonorrhoeae bacterium. If they detect this bacterium, it means you have gonorrhea. They might take a sample from your vagina, cervix, urethra(urine passage), rectum or throat. Urine (pee) test. This involves collecting urine in a specimen container, then taken to the lab for testing.

TargetRLF1: Gonorrhea can be traced back to the earliest records of the human race even if Albert Neisser first described the bacteria gonococcus in 1879. The Romans, Jews and Arabs all have documents referring to gonorrhea and each society had their own description of symptoms and treatment. The origins of Gonorrhoe can be Traced Back to the Old Testament in the books of Leviticus and Genesis where it is described as a contagious, continuous emission of semen and painful erection. The Romano physician Galen in 130 AD described The disease as an "involuntary escape of semen". The word itself derives from the Greek, meaning "the flow of seed" (gono: seed, rhea: flow).

Here are some key points to consider: Contraceptive After Conception, Importance of Prenatal Care, Safe and Effective Contraception, Emergency Contraception , Termination of Pregnancy. In summary, the use of contraceptiones after conception is Generally Not Relevan, As Contraceptivies are designed to prevent the pregnancy before it occurs. If pregnancy is confirmed or suspected, seeking prenatal care and discussing options with a healthcare provider is crucial to ensure the health and well-being of both the pregnant individual and the developing fetus. Future contraception options can also be discussed to prevent unintended pregnancies.

In babies, eye infection can be redness , pain, orness, wounds in the eye, tearing, swelling eyelids and pus discharge. which can lead to blindness if untreated. Once the baby is born, it is given a preventive ARV syrup and follow

TargetRLF1: Human immunodeficiency virus is a zoonosis – an infection that originated in animals and passed into human hosts. It is closely related to the simian immunodeficient virus (SIV) found in chimpanzees in central Africa. To date, the earliest known case of HIV-1 infection in human blood is from a sample taken in 1959 from another man who'd died in Kinshasa in what was then the Belgian Congo. SIV probably entered the human population as a result of hunting chimpanzes.It is plausible that transmission could have occurred through a hunter being wounded and blood from  a chimpanzae entering his bloodstream. Chimpanzee avoid humans wherever possible, so it is likely that SIVcpz crossed into humans as the result of human encroachment into forest areas. The origin of the Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV) has been a subject of scientific research and debate since the virus was identified in the 1980s. It came to global attention after doctors started receiving reports of infection

TargetRLF1: No, urine from rabbits or any other animals does not cure HIV/AIDS. HIV (Human Immunodeficiency Virus) is a complex virus that affects the human immune system. While there have been numerous myths, misconceptions, and alternative medicine claims regarding potential cures or treatments for HIV AIDS, none of them have been scientifically proven to be effective. It is crucial to rely on evidence-based medical treatments and information when dealing with HIV/AID. Alternative or unproven treatments can be ineffective, harmful, and potentially dangerous.

In summary, while PEP can reduce the Risk of HIV transmission after a possible eexposition, it will not an substitute for safer sex practices, including condom use. It's crucial to maintain precautions during The PEP Treatment Period and beyond to protect yourself and your sexual partners from HIV and other STIs. If you have questions or concerns about sexual health and HIV prevention, consult with a health provider or a sexual health counselor for guidance tailored to your specific situation.

TargetRLF1: PMS, or premenstrual syndrome, refers to a combination of physical and emotional symptoms that many people experience before their period. Symptoms can include mood swings, irritability, fatigue, bloating, breast tenderness, food cravings, and headaches. Here are some ways to manage PMS symptoms: Healthy Diet: Eating a balanced diet with plenty of fruits, vegetables, and whole grains can help. Exercise: Regular exercise can improve mood and reduce symptoms like bloating and fatigue. Stress Management: Practicing relaxation techniques like deep breathing or meditation can help reduce stress and improve mood. Over-the-counter medications: Pain relievers like ibuprofen or naproxen can help with cramps and headache. Always follow the instructions on the label. If your PMSsymptoms are severe and affecting your daily life, talk to another healthcare provider. They can offer advice and treatment options.

TargetRLF1: Human immunodeficiency virus is an infection that originated in animals and passed into human hosts. It is closely related to the simian immunodeficient virus (SIV) found in chimpanzees in central Africa. To date, the earliest known case of HIV-1 infection in human blood is from a sample taken in 1959 from another man who died in Kinshasa in what was then the Belgian Congo. SIV probably entered the human population as a result of hunting chimpanzes. It was plausible that transmission could have occurred through a hunter being wounded and blood from the chimpanzae entering his bloodstream. The origin of the Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV) has been a subject of scientific research and debate since the virus was identified in the 1980s.

TargetRLF1: A chlamydia also known as Chlamydial Genitourinary infection test detects the bacteria that cause chlamya, a sexually transmitted infection (STI). This test tells you if you have the chlamydi infection so that you can get treat treatment and avoid complications. Your provider takes a sample of urine or secretions from your vagina, penis or rectum and sends it to a lab for testing using a test called NAATs (short for nucleic acid amplification tests), which is very accurate and easy to take. Results are usually ready in a day. Less often, providers use a cell culture to test for chlamudia. In this case, your provider swabs your vagina , urethra or anus to collect another sample of fluid and cells. They send the cells to another lab to test. If bacteria are present, they will grow, which indicates an infection. Providers may choose this type of chlamanydia test to detect infections in the anus. Or they may use another cell culture

TargetRLF1: Ikiwa kondomu itapasuka au kupasuka wakati wa kujamiiana, inaweza kusababisha hatari ya mimba na uwezekano wa kuambukizwa magonjwa ya zinaa (STIs), ikiwa ni pamoja na virusi vya ukimwi. Hivi ndivyo unapaswa kufanya ikiwa kondomu Itapasuka: Acha Mara Moja, Toa kwa Makini, Uzuiaji Mimba wa Dharura, Upimaji wa magojwa ya zinaanya, Panga kwa Wakati Ujao, Badilisha Kondomu. Kumbuka kwamba kondomu ni nzuri sana inapotumiwa kwa usahihi na mara kwa mara, lakini hakuna njia ya kuzuia mimba ambayo ni 100%. Katika tukio la kukatika kwa kondomu, kuchukua hatua za haraka ni muhimu ili kupunguza hatari.

TargetRLF1: Kugusa: Kugusa kunahusisha kuweka mikono yako, sehemu nyingine za mwili, au vinyago vya ngono kwa mwenzi wako’s uke, uume, au mkundu. Hatari pekee inayowezekana itakuwa ikiwa maji ya mwili kutoka kwa mtu aliye na Virusi Vya Ukimwi yatagusa utando wa mucous au tishu zilizoharibiwa za mtu asiye na Virusia Vyaukimwi. Utando wa mcous hupatikana ndani ya puru, uke, ufunguzi wa uume , na mdomo. Tishu zilizoharibi wa zinaweza kujumuisha kupunguzwa, vidonda, au majeraha wazi. Unaweza kupata au kusambaza magonjwa mengine ya zinaa (kama vile papillomavirus ya binadamu au HPV, malengelenge ya sehemu za siri, na kaswende) kupitia mguso wa ngozi hadi ngozi. Ukigusa mkundu wa mtu na kupata kinyesi kwenye mikono au vidole vyako, unaweza pia kupata ama kusambaza HIVis A na hepatitis B. Kuambukizwa na vimelea kama vile

Zungumza na mtoa huduma wako wa afya ikiwa hutumii matibabu au ikiwa unatatizika kuvumilia.

Ni muhimu kutambua kwamba sio vijana wote wanaoishi na virusizi vya  Ukivyo watakabiliwa na changamoto inayofanana, na wengi wanaweza kuishi maisha yenye afya, yinye kuridhisha na huduma za afya

TargetRLF1: Ni muhimu kuelewa kwamba hakuna njia ya kubainisha hali yako ya Ukimwi bila kupima Ukimwi. Ukimwi ni virusi ambavyo sio kila mara husababisha dalili za haraka, na vinaweza kubaki katika mwili wako kwa miaka mingi bila dalili zozote zinazoonekana. Kutegemea dalili au mawazo kuhusu hali ya Ukivyo ya mwenzi wako kunaweza kusababisha kutoelewana na hitimisho lisilo sahihi. Njia pekee ya kujua hali yako yo Ukimwi kwa sahihi sana, na kuna aina tofauti za vipimo vinavyopatikana, ikiwa ni pamoja na vipimo vya haraka ambavyo hutoa matokeo kwa muda wa dakika 20. Upimaji ni wa siri, na katika maeneo mengi, unaweza kufanywa bila kujulikana.

TargetRLF1: Super gonorrhea ni neno linalotumiwa kuelezea aina za bakteria Neisseria gonorrhoeae ambazo zimekuwa sugu kwa matibabu ya kawaida, kama vile viuavijasumu kama vilene azithromycin na ceftriaxone. Kisonono chenyewe, kiwe kinasababishwa na aina sugu ya dawa au la, kinaweza kuwa na madhara makubwa kiafya kikiachwa bila kutibiwa. Ingawa kisonono kwa kawaida si hatari, inaweza kusababisha matatizo makubwa ya kiafya, hasa iwapo itasambaa katika sehemu nyingine za mwili. Yafuatayo ni baadhi ya matatizo yanayoweza kusababishwa: Ugonjwa wa Kuvimba kwa Pelvic (PID), Utasa, Kuenea kwa Viungo Vingine, Kuongezeka kwa Hatari ya virusi vya ukimwi, Maambukizi ya Gonococcal (DGI), Matatizo ya Watoto wachanga. PEP kwa kawaida hutumika katika hali ambapo kumekuwa na mfiduo unaojulikana au unaoshukiwa kuwa katika hatari kubwa ya Ukimwi. Ni muhimu kutambua kwamba PEP si mbadala wa mbinu za mara kwa mara za kuchukuaia Ukimwi, kama vile matumizi ya kondomu na kuzuie pre-exposure prophylaxis (PrEP) kwa watu walio katika Hatari inayoendelea ya KuambukizwaUkimwi. PrEP ni dawa ya kizuia ambazo inachukuliwa kwa kabla ya kuambikizwa Ukiwi, wakati PEP Inachukuliwe baada Ya Mfiduo Maalum wa Hatari Mkubwa. Ikiwa unaamini kuwa umeambukizwaukimwi, tafuta matibabu mara moja ili kujadili kama PEPinafaa kwa hali yako.

TargetRLF1: Ukimwi (Virusi vya Upungufu wa Kinga ya Mwili) ni virusi vinavyoshambulia mfumo wa kinga, haswa seli za CD4 (T seli), ambazo husaidia mfumo w kinga kupigana na maambukizo. Baada ya muda, ikiwa haitatibiwa, Ukimwi inaweza kusababisha hali inayoitwa UKIMWI (Acquired Immunodeficiency Syndrome).UKIMWI ni hatua ya mwisho ya maambukizi ya Ukimwi, ambayo hutokea wakati mfumo Wa kinga umeharibiwa sana. Ili kugundulika kuwa na AKIMWI, idadi ya seli z CD4 ya mtu hushuka chini ya kiwango fulani, na/au wanapata magonjwa nyemelezi moja au zaidi au saratani fulani.

TargetRLF1: Neno "kienyeji" kwa ujumla hurejelea desturi za kimapokeo au za kiasili, ambazo mara nyingi huhusishwa na utamaduni au eneo fulani. Dawa na mazoea ya jadi yanaweza kutofautiana sana, na ufanisi wao unategemea mbinu maalum na tiba zinazotumiwa. Baadhi ya mila za kitamaduni zinaweza kuwa zimepitishwa kwa vizazi na zinaweza Kuwa na msingi fulani katika dawa za asili au tiba zingine za asila, wakati zingine zinaweza kutegemea ushirikina au imani za kitamduni. Linapokuja suala la kutibu magonjwa au kuzuia mimba, ni muhimu kukabiliana na mazoEa haya kwa tahadhari na mashaka. Ingawa baadhi ya tiba za kita maduni zinweza kuwa na sifa za kimatibabu na umuhimu wa kitamadunia, huenda zisiwe salama au madhubuti kila wakati. Ufanisi wa matibabu yoyote, iwe ya jadi au ya kisasa, inapaswa kuungwa mkono na ushahidi wa kisayansi.

TargetRLF1: Ndiyo, tiba ya kurefusha maisha (ART) inapunguza kiwango cha kuzidisha virusi vya ukimwi (kujirudia) katika mwili. ART ni mchanganyiko wa dawa zilizoundwa kukandamiza uzazi wa Virusi vya Ukimwi (virusi vya usaimwi) na kupunguza kasi ya kuendelea kwake. Hivi ndivyo ART inavyofanya kazi ili kufikia hili: Kulenga Hatua Mbalimbali za Kurudufisha, Kuzuia Reverse Transcriptase, KuzUIA Protease, Kuzuhia Muunganisho, Kuzuria Uzalishaji Mpya wa VirusI, Kurejesha Utendaji Kazi wa Kinga, Kukandamiza Dalili na Kupunguza Matatizo, na Kuzuia Maambukizi. Kwa ujumla, ART ni musingi wa udhibiti wa virusi vinavyo  Ukimwi, na ina jukumu muhimu katika kudhibiti uzaji wa virus

TargetRLF1: Kuishi na virusi vya ukimwi (Virusi vya Upungufu wa Kinga ya Mwili) kunaweza kudhibitiwa, na watu walio na virusia vya  Ukimwi wanaweza kuishi maisha yenye afya, ywenye kuridhisha kwa huduma za matibabu zinazofaa, usaidizi, na uchaguzi wa mtindo wa maisha. Kuishi nad virusi vinavyo ukivyo kunahitaji huduma ya kibinafsi inayoendelea, usimamizi wa matibabu, na usaidiz kutoka kwa watoa huduma z afya , marafiki na familia. Ni muhimu kukumbuka kuwa watu wengi walio na Virusi vya Ukimwi huishi maisha marefu na yinye afya kwa matibabu yanayofaa na kujitolea kudumisha ustawi wa jumla. Ikiwa una maswali au wasiwasi kuhusu kuishi na virali vya

TargetRLF1: Ikiwa unajali kuhusu faragha na unapendelea kupima Ukimwi katika kituo tofauti ambapo kuna uwezekano mdogo wa kutambuliwa, kuna chaguzi kadhaa za kuzingatia. Unaweza kutembelea kituo tafauti cha huduma ya afya au zahanati katika eneo lako inayotoa huduma za kupimaUkimwi. Tafuta kliniki za afya za umma, vituo vya afya vya jamii, au wahudumu wa afya wa kibinafsi ambapo unaweza kupokea upimaji wa siri. Maeneo mengine hutoa upi maji wa Ukimwi bila kukutambulisha, ambapo huhitaji kutoa taarifa zako za kibinadamu. Tovuti hizi hutumia vitambulishi vya kipekee ili kulinda faragha yako. Kuna vifaa vya kupima Usimwi vya nyumbani vilivyoidhinishwa vinavyopatikana kwa kununuliwa kwenye maduka ya dawa au mtandaoni.

TargetRLF1: Vijana walio na virusi vya ukimwi wanakabiliwa na seti ya kipekee ya changamoto zinazoweza kuathiri ustawi wao wa kimwili, kihisia na kijamii. Changamoto hizi zinaweza kutofautiana kulingana na hali ya mtu binafsi, lakini baadhi ya masuala ya kawaida yanayowakabili vijana waliona virusi vinavyo  Ukimwi ni pamoja na: Unyanyapaa na Ubaguzi, Masuala ya Afya ya Akili, Ufichuzi na Maswala ya Faragha, Usimamizi wa Matibabu, Afya Ya Ujinsia na Uzazi, Mahusiano ya Rika, Maswala Ya Kielimu na Ajira, Kutengwa na Upweke, Matumizi ya Dawa, Upatikanaji wa Matunzo, Changamoto za Kinga, Mienendo ya Wazazi na Familia, Athari za Kihisia za Utambuzi. Ni muhimu kutambua kwamba sio vijana wote wanaoishi na virusizi vya  Ukivyo watakabiliwa na changamoto inayofanana, na wengi wanaweza kuishi maisha yenye afya, yinye kuridhisha na huduma za afya
